SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.depressurization

Class NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.depressurization.NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel extends Object implements Serializable
Non-equilibrium (two-temperature) blowdown model for a fire-exposed pressurized vessel.

Conventional blowdown models assume a single fluid temperature. Experiments and rigorous simulations (Haque, Richardson and Saville's BLOWDOWN; the partial-pressure / non-equilibrium method, NEM) show that during depressurizing fire exposure the gas (vapour) space and the liquid space evolve at markedly different temperatures: the vapour can superheat by several hundred kelvin while the boiling liquid stays pinned near its saturation temperature. This gas–liquid temperature bifurcation is missed by single-temperature models and is the governing input for low-metal- temperature and wall-rupture assessments.

The model carries two temperature zones (vapour and liquid) at a shared, vapour-controlled pressure. Each zone is a child SystemInterface (extracted with phaseToSystem) flashed independently each step for its properties. Heat enters through a lumped wall (split between the unwetted vapour-side and the wetted liquid-side area), the wetted wall drives nucleate boiling (Rohsenow, VesselHeatTransferCorrelations.rohsenowNucleateBoilingHeatFlux(double, double, double, double, double, double, double, double, double, double)), and a series-resistance interfacial term couples the two zones. Boil-off transfers moles from the liquid zone to the vapour zone, and the vapour discharges through a choked / subsonic orifice.

This is a screening-grade NEM: it captures the qualitative gas–liquid bifurcation and depressurization trend rather than a rigorous coupled VLE march, and all flashes are guarded so a transient property failure degrades a single step instead of aborting the run.

References: Haque, Richardson, Saville et al. (1992), Trans. IChemE 70(B); Mahgerefteh et al. blowdown studies; API STD 521 §4.3.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • MIN_ZONE_TEMPERATURE_K

      private static final double MIN_ZONE_TEMPERATURE_K
      Lower physical floor for any zone temperature in K. The explicit integration is stiff for the wetted-wall boiling coupling; this floor keeps a divergent step from producing an unphysical (negative absolute) temperature in a screening run.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel

      public NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel(SystemInterface fluid, double vesselVolume, double orificeDiameter, double dischargeCoefficient, double backPressure)
      Constructs a non-equilibrium blowdown model.
      Parameters:
      fluid - initial two-phase fluid in the vessel; mixing rule must already be set
      vesselVolume - internal vessel volume in m³; must be positive
      orificeDiameter - blowdown orifice diameter in m; must be positive
      dischargeCoefficient - orifice discharge coefficient (typically 0.61 to 0.85)
      backPressure - downstream absolute pressure in Pa
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if fluid is null or a dimension is not positive

  • Method Details

    • setFireExposure

      public NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel setFireExposure(double emissivity, double flameTempK, double convCoeff, double exposedAreaM2)
      Configures the radiative-convective fire boundary condition acting on the wall.
      Parameters:
      emissivity - effective flame emissivity from 0 to 1
      flameTempK - effective radiating flame temperature in K; must be positive
      convCoeff - flame-to-wall convective film coefficient in W/(m²·K)
      exposedAreaM2 - fire-exposed external area in m²; must be positive
      Returns:
      this model for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if flameTempK or exposedAreaM2 is not positive

    • setWall

      public NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel setWall(double wallMass, double wallSpecificHeat)
      Configures the lumped wall thermal mass.
      Parameters:
      wallMass - total wall metal mass in kg; must be positive
      wallSpecificHeat - metal specific heat in J/(kg·K)
      Returns:
      this model for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if wallMass is not positive

    • setInsideGasCoefficient

      public NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel setInsideGasCoefficient(double insideGasCoefficient)
      Sets the inside vapour-side wall film coefficient.
      Parameters:
      insideGasCoefficient - vapour-side film coefficient in W/(m²·K); must be positive
      Returns:
      this model for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if insideGasCoefficient is not positive

    • setInterfacial

      public NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel setInterfacial(double coefficient, double areaM2)
      Sets the gas–liquid interfacial heat-transfer coefficient and interface area.
      Parameters:
      coefficient - interfacial heat-transfer coefficient in W/(m²·K); must be positive
      areaM2 - interface area in m²; must be positive
      Returns:
      this model for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if an argument is not positive

    • setLatentHeat

      public NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel setLatentHeat(double latentHeat)
      Sets the latent heat of vaporization used for the boil-off mass transfer.
      Parameters:
      latentHeat - latent heat in J/kg; must be positive
      Returns:
      this model for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if latentHeat is not positive

    • setTimeStep

      public NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel setTimeStep(double dt)
      Sets the integration time step.
      Parameters:
      dt - time step in s; must be positive
      Returns:
      this model for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dt is not positive

    • setMaxTime

      public NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel setMaxTime(double tMax)
      Sets the maximum simulation time.
      Parameters:
      tMax - maximum time in s; must be positive
      Returns:
      this model for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if tMax is not positive

    • setStopPressure

      public NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel setStopPressure(double pMin)
      Sets the stop pressure.
      Parameters:
      pMin - stop pressure in Pa absolute; must be positive
      Returns:
      this model for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pMin is not positive

    • run

      public NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel.NemResult run()
      Runs the non-equilibrium blowdown transient.
      Returns:
      a NonEquilibriumBlowdownModel.NemResult with the vapour-zone and liquid-zone temperature, pressure and mass trajectories
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the initial fluid does not flash into a coexisting vapour and liquid zone

    • massOf

      private static double massOf(SystemInterface system)
      Computes the total mass of a fluid in kg from its mole inventory and molar mass.
      Parameters:
      system - the fluid system
      Returns:
      total mass in kg

    • scaleMoles

      private static void scaleMoles(SystemInterface system, double factor)
      Scales the total mole inventory of a fluid by a factor while preserving composition.
      Parameters:
      system - the fluid system to scale
      factor - multiplicative scaling factor; values not greater than zero, NaN or equal to one are ignored

    • transferMoles

      private static void transferMoles(SystemInterface source, SystemInterface target, double massKg, double sourceMolarMass)
      Transfers a mass of fluid from a source system to a target system using the source composition.
      Parameters:
      source - the donor fluid (liquid zone)
      target - the receiving fluid (vapour zone)
      massKg - mass to transfer in kg; non-positive values are ignored
      sourceMolarMass - source molar mass in kg/mol
